seoTitle: Mount Clarence vs Mount Elphinstone — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Clarence and Mount Elphinstone. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Clarence vs Mount Elphinstone: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Clarence and Mount Elphinstone.
heading: Mount Clarence vs Mount Elphinstone — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Clarence, Western Australia and Mount Elphinstone, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Clarence has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Mount Elphinstone (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Clarence or Mount Elphinstone safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Clarence has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Mount Elphinstone scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Clarence scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Elphinstone scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Clarence or Mount Elphinstone?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Clarence has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
